mission.title: oauth2-proxy: Client secret is not required for OIDC with PKCE enabled
mission.description: Client secret is not required for OIDC with PKCE enabled. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current oauth2-proxy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your oauth2-proxy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
helm list -n oauth2-proxy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working oauth2-proxy installation.

steps.title: Review oauth2-proxy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant oauth2-proxy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get configmap -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=oauth2-proxy
```
Trying to use oauth2-proxy with Okta PKCE as per (this)[https://developer.okta.com/blog/2019/08/22/okta-authjs-pkce] article.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Client secret is not required for OIDC with PKCE enabled
steps.description:
I think in the initial implementation we decided to defer this to simplify working on it. If anyone has time to work on making this work and making the client secret optional then a PR would be welcomed.
```yaml
image:
        tag: v7.3.0
      config:
        clientID: blahblahblah
        clientSecret: ""
        cookieSecret: blahblahblahblahblah
      extraArgs:
        provider: oidc
        redirect-url: /oauth2/callback
        oidc-issuer-url: https://somedomain.oktapreview.com/oauth2/default
        code-challenge-method: S256
        email-domain: "*"
        skip-provider-button: true
        set-authorization-header: true
        set-xauthrequest: true
        scope: openid profile email groups
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n oauth2-proxy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=oauth2-proxy
kubectl get events -n oauth2-proxy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Client secret is not required for OIDC with PKCE enabled" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I think in the initial implementation we decided to defer this to simplify working on it. If anyone has time to work on making this work and making the client secret optional then a PR would be welcomed.
codeSnippets:
image:
        tag: v7.3.0
      config:
        clientID: blahblahblah
        clientSecret: ""
        cookieSecret: blahblahblahblahblah
      extraArgs:
        provider: oidc
        redirect-url: /oauth2/callback
        oidc-issuer-url: https://somedomain.oktapreview.com/oauth2/default
        code-challenge-method: S256
        email-domain: "*"
        skip-provider-button: true
        set-authorization-header: true
        set-xauthrequest: true
        scope: openid profile email groups

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with oauth2-proxy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
